assets.url: inline-svg:VML wordmark (header sprite symbol, viewBox 0 0 62 19, fill currentColor)
inline-svg:VML wordmark rendered white-on-black in footer (currentColor #ffffff)

gaps: No designed action/link color found: links render in browser-default #0000ee (UA stylesheet). Primary set to the dominant brand color #000000 (black). colorUsage.link captures the footer/nav pattern (white #ffffff link text on black), the only intentional link styling observed.
No brand semantic colors (success/warning/error/info) detected. #32ae88 green and #3860be blue belong to the third-party OneTrust cookie widget, not the brand, and were dropped.
Single type family only (SnowflakeSans); no distinct body-vs-heading family and no monospace family. Weight 600 (SnowflakeSans-Medium) is loaded and used by h4, so it is included in weights.
Decorative accent-tile set present (#983f03 amber, #546232 olive, #723065 purple, #ae7408 amber) beyond the single 'accent' slot filled by #104b68; schema allows only one accent string.
No radius:none (0) signal in tokens; smallest observed radius is ~2px. The card component recipe does use borderRadius 0px, carried verbatim in components.card.

Manifest reconciliation: manifest theme_color and background_color are both #ffffff, which map to palette neutral.0 (the brand's white canvas / colorUsage.background), NOT to primary #000000. Consistent with VML's white-base, black-ink system, so no palette change was made.
darkMode is null: no prefers-color-scheme dark rules were crawled. The white-on-black footer and inverted logo are per-section design, not a global dark theme/toggle.

Breakpoints collapsed from 25 raw min/max-width values to the 5 recognizable device tiers present in the raw set (600/768/1024/1280/1440px); many intermediate values (425/426, 599/600, 768/769, 896/897, 979/980, 1023/1024, 1179/1180) are off-by-one min/max boundary pairs, not distinct tiers.

Third-party OneTrust-hosted VML logo (cdn.cookielaw.org/.../vml-logo.png) dropped from assets: it is the brand mark but delivered via the cookie-consent widget CDN, not a first-party brand asset; the first-party SVG logos cover the role.
